hidden_constraints.oracle_response: big_integer maps to number; binary maps to string; unknown maps to either Record<string, unknown> or never depending on context.

trigger_keywords: what was inside the enum string
how did the escape character get quoted
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The exact string was 'small_unicode_escape_character' with backslash escapes preserved within the generated declaration.

trigger_keywords: how are relationship fields outputted in json mode
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Output format is exactly 'name=<related_entity_key>' followed by 'type=<relationship_array_of_objects>' on new lines.

context_pointers.vague_reference: check how global enums are constructed in the namespace container
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The system outputs a const wrapped enum class with a backing type alias that maps to the string-derived template.

context_pointers.vague_reference: refer to the global wrapper pattern used for all type definitions
context_pointers.actual_meaning: All interfaces and enums exported via generate_definitions_with_namespace are stripped of the global namespace prefix and re-exported top-level.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_model_discovery.json.description: Discovers all eligible sample data models in the project or narrows discovery to a requested model.

feature1_2_model_inspection.json.description: Inspects a sample data model and reports whether schema attributes and relationships are available for generation.

feature2_1_default_interface_output.json.description: Generates a TypeScript interface for a sample user-shaped data model, including columns, computed fields, relationships, and literal union constants.

feature2_2_complex_type_mapping_output.j.description: Generates a TypeScript interface for a model containing many database field categories and maps each field to the corresponding TypeScript type.

cases.expected_output:
export interface Complex {
  // columns
  id: number
  big_integer: number
  binary: unknown
  boolean: boolean
  char: string
  date_time: string
  immutable_date_time: string
  immutable_custom_date_time: string
  date: string
  immutable_date: string
  decimal: number
  double: number
  enum: string
  float: number
  integer: number
  ip_address: string
  json: Record<string, unknown>
  jsonb: Record<string, unknown>
  long_text: string
  mac_address: string
  medium_integer: number
  medium_text: string
  small_integer: number
  string: string
  casted_uppercase_string: unknown
  text: string
  time: string
  timestamp: string
  year: number
  uuid: string
  ulid: string
  created_at: string | null
  updated_at: string | null
  deleted_at: string | null
}

feature2_3_global_namespace_output.json.description: Wraps generated interfaces in a configured global namespace when namespace output is requested.

feature2_4_json_metadata_output.json.description: Generates structured JSON metadata for a sample model when JSON output is requested, including interface fields, relations, and enum metadata.

feature3_1_enum_rendering.json.description: Renders backed string constants as TypeScript declarations while preserving descriptions and escaped string values.

feature3_2_relationship_rendering.json.description: Renders relationship fields as TypeScript properties or structured field metadata, with optionality and plural resource-name variants.

feature4_1_generation_options.json.description: Applies generation switches that alter the emitted interface, including editable subsets, enum style, plural names, relation inclusion, hidden fields, date object timestamps, nullable optionality, and API resource wrapping.

feature4_2_type_mapping_configuration.js.description: Maps raw type tokens using default and user-provided mappings, including nullable tokens, unknown tokens, timestamp date-object mapping, and custom overrides.

feature5_1_error_normalization.json.description: Reports language-neutral error categories when generation cannot find models, a type token is empty, or an accessor selector cannot be resolved.

feature5_2_accessor_resolution.json.description: Recognizes both modern and legacy computed attribute selectors on a sample model.

_constraints.input_condition: Request action is 'discover_models' and subject is 'all'
_constraints.output_rule: Output a count line followed by one 'model=<name>' line for each discovered concrete model

_constraints.input_condition: Request action is 'discover_models' and subject is a specific string (e.g., 'user')
_constraints.output_rule: Output a count line followed by one 'model=<name>' line for the specific requested model only, preserving the same output shape as 'all'

_constraints.input_condition: Request action is 'inspect_model' and subject corresponds to a concrete model with schema
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'result=inspected', followed by 'table=<name>', 'attributes=<count>', and 'relations=<count>' on subsequent lines

_constraints.input_condition: Request action is 'inspect_model' and subject is abstract or missing schema information
Request action is 'generate_definitions', subject is valid, no extra options provided
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'export interface <Name>' block with comments '// columns', '// mutators', '// relations'. Include persisted columns, computed attributes, relationships, and string-literal constant definitions dependent on generated enums.

_constraints.input_condition: Field has nullability metadata (e.g. nullable column)
_constraints.output_rule: Output type as '<name> | null'. Optional fields use '?' suffix before type.

_constraints.input_condition: Database field belongs to known categories (integer, string, boolean, date/time, json, etc.)
_constraints.output_rule: Map database field category to specific TypeScript primitive or object type (number, string, boolean, Record<string, unknown>, string, etc.)

_constraints.input_condition: Request action is 'generate_definitions' with config.global_namespace set
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'export {}'. Output 'declare global'. Wrap all interface and enum definitions inside 'namespace <GlobalNamespace}'

_constraints.input_condition: Request action is 'generate_definitions' with options.json set to true
_constraints.output_rule: Output JSON object with 'interfaces' (array of objects with 'name' and 'type'), 'relations' (array of arrays mapping resource types to metadata), and 'enums' (array of objects with 'name' and 'type')

_constraints.input_condition: Request action is 'generate_definitions' with options.json=true and options.use_enums=true
_constraints.output_rule: Inside the 'enums' array, output an object with 'name' (the enum constant name) and 'type' containing the full generated enum declaration text with escaped newlines and quotes.

_constraints.input_condition: Request action is 'generate_definitions' with options.fillable_subset=true and options.fillable_suffix set
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'export interface <Name>Editable' containing fields specified by the suffix. Append 'export type <Name>Editable = Pick<User, ...>'
Output 'export const enum Roles'. Append 'export type RolesEnum = `\${Roles}`'
Output 'type=<mapped_value>' reflecting the lookup from mappings object or defaults

_constraints.input_condition: Request action involves 'generate_definitions' (or specific subject) and subject/model cannot be found or is abstract

_constraints.output_rule: Output 'accessor=<selector>' and 'result=found' if the selector represents an accessible computed attribute

hide.replacement: a specific compound value based on DB type and whether it's a boolean flag — consult schema docs
